page: Microsoft Azure E160as_v7 is an Easv7-series virtual machine configured with 160 dedicated vCPUs and 1280.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an AMD processor using the x86_64 architecture, this instance runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The memory-to-vCPU ratio stands at 8.0 GB per core, positioning the server within the memory-optimized category.

This server configuration does not include local storage, meaning all storage operations rely on external network-attached storage, though the instance is Premium Storage capable. Additionally, the E160as_v7 does not feature any graphics processing units or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. These hardware omissions represent a tradeoff, making the instance less suitable for local-disk-intensive workloads or GPU-accelerated tasks, while focusing resources entirely on memory capacity and compute density.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E160as_v7 offers high resource density for enterprise-scale workloads requiring massive memory footprints without the overhead of local NVMe drives or GPU hardware. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities. This configuration is designed for memory-intensive workloads such as large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching systems, enterprise resource planning applications, and large-scale data analytics.
description: The Microsoft Azure E160as_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Easv7 family, featuring 160 dedicated vCPUs on an x86_64 AMD processor architecture. Managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance provides 1280.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per core memory-to-vCPU ratio. This configuration lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, relying instead on external Premium Storage capabilities for data persistence. By omitting local disks and accelerators, the server maximizes resource density and cost efficiency specifically for memory-bound applications. The E160as_v7 is structured for large-scale enterprise workloads, including in-memory databases, large-scale data analytics, and memory-intensive business applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E160as_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 160 dedicated AMD vCPUs, 1280 GB of RAM, and Hyper-V virtualization, designed for large-scale enterprise databases.
